content: Existing comparisons of curiosity-driven reinforcement learning methods (ICM vs RND vs intrinsic curious agents) reveal that performance differences often stem from how each method encodes unexpected memory; this observation can be leveraged to build a 2025 SOTA active learning agent that uses method-specific intrinsic curiosity modules to dynamically prune and prioritize episodic memory based on unexpectedness. This creates a self-tuning memory system that eliminates the need for hand-designed active sampling rules.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
